scenario_results.reason: Normal royalty administration produced a source-linked franchise royalty workflow summary covering agreement/FDD context, sales report, payment match, approved credit, AR status, and audit trail.
Missing agreement amendment, POS detail, signed report, payment receipt, or adjustment support correctly blocked the package with owners, due dates, and retrieval hooks.
Unsupported changes to rate, gross-sales exclusion, unit status, late fee, or credit were refused and routed to official record or financial approval owners.
Conflicting FDD, agreement, portal, AR, and POS records were escalated instead of reconciled informally.
Retention, privacy, legal-hold, and access conflicts triggered governance escalation and refusal of unsafe franchisee financial-data distribution.
Official balance approval, default certification, legal-hold release, write-off approval, and audit adjustment approval were refused and routed.
Multi-unit royalty cycle terminology and artifacts produced a unit-level royalty completeness summary with fee, remittance, dispute, and evidence controls.
Legal interpretation, revenue recognition, tax advice, collections strategy, and audit finding approval were rejected as adjacent-lane work.
Missing unit roster, period, fee type, agreement, FDD, POS source, payment hook, approval matrix, or source hierarchy produced low-confidence retrieval blockers.
Conflicting public, tenant, contract, accounting, and AR guidance was escalated for human precedence resolution.
Tenant-specific brand, fee schedule, gross-sales, POS, ad-fund, area-rep, and retention differences were preserved as configuration assumptions.
Generic donor/member contribution framing was rejected and converted into franchise-specific royalty evidence prerequisites.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for franchise royalty evidence, records maintenance, retention/access governance, boundary safety, industry specificity, and tenant adaptation.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, records-documentation-and-administration, and cross-industry abilities.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based; no retained implementation is claimed.
